fault_hypothesis: The while loop condition on line 56 uses `>` (strictly greater than) instead of `>=`, so when `len(order) == capacity` the loop body is never entered and no eviction occurs.

constraints.intent: After the while guard is evaluated, if len(order)==capacity the loop should be entered but with '>' it is not; this expression being true indicates the bug is present (loop was skipped when it should not have been).
After each loop iteration the list should be strictly under capacity if eviction is working correctly.
The returned list must be strictly under capacity after eviction sweep when the input length was >= capacity.
Initially total_evicted is 0; if the loop is never entered (due to wrong condition) it stays 0 even when eviction was needed.
